_meta.description: Frozen snapshot of WatcherEventBuilder emit sites in the Rust mirror repo for component ∈ {combat, encounter}. Used by tests/telemetry/test_rust_span_name_parity.py (Story 42-4 AC3). Each entry pins a Rust emit site to a Python span constant, or documents a deliberate deviation.
_meta.extraction_method: gh api search/code + grep for WatcherEventBuilder::new + field('event'|'action', ...)
_meta.convention: Python SPAN_* span name == Rust component + '.' + field('event'|'action') value, EXCEPT where python_deviation is set.
_meta.update_procedure: Re-run the gh-api extraction against the Rust mirror; add/remove entries and update snapshot_date. Commit fixture changes alongside any Python SPAN_* additions.

rust_events.python_deviation: Rust uses .state. infix redundantly with component=encounter; Python flattens for span readability. Load-bearing consumer: GM panel query pattern 'encounter.*'. Documented deviation.

MISSING IN PYTHON — no SPAN_ENCOUNTER_ESCALATED constant. Rust emits on chase→combat escalation (encounter.rs::escalate_to_combat). GM panel query pattern expects this span. AC3 REQUIRES: Python adds SPAN_ENCOUNTER_ESCALATED = 'encounter.escalated'.
Rust uses .state. infix; Python flattens. Documented deviation.
Rust uses .state. infix; Python flattens. Documented deviation.

MISSING IN PYTHON — no SPAN_ENCOUNTER_RESOLVED_BY_TROPE constant. Rust emits distinct from encounter.state.resolved when resolution is trope-driven (AC5). GM panel distinguishes these in filters. AC3 REQUIRES: Python adds SPAN_ENCOUNTER_RESOLVED_BY_TROPE = 'encounter.resolved_by_trope' OR documents why trope-resolution collapses into SPAN_ENCOUNTER_RESOLVED.
Python uses 'confrontation_initiated' verbiage inherited from older story 3.4 scaffold; Rust uses 'created'. Documented naming deviation; behaviour is identical.
MISSING IN PYTHON — no SPAN_ENCOUNTER_BEAT_DISPATCHED constant. Precedes beat_applied; observable lifecycle event. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — no SPAN_ENCOUNTER_STAT_CHECK_RESOLVED constant. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — no equivalent. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — beat dispatched but no ConfrontationDef. Error-path observability. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — beat dispatched but no active encounter. Error-path observability. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — unknown beat id. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — Edge/composure system break event (ADR-014 Diamonds and Coal relevant). AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — fail-loud on unknown encounter_type. Maps to 42-1 AC5 (ValidationError). Observability hook for the same failure. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — escalation path observability. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — escalation path observability. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — encounter-scoped gold mutation. Possibly out-of-scope for Phase 3 (narrative currency). AC3: document scope deferral if intentional.
MISSING IN PYTHON — inverse of encounter.gold_delta. AC3: document scope deferral if intentional.
MISSING IN PYTHON — narrator tried to re-declare encounter. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — narrator re-declared same encounter. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — encounter replaced before any beat fired. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — prompt-zone injection observability. AC3 REQUIRES: add or document scope deferral.
MISSING IN PYTHON — counts beats dispatched per encounter. Action-style field rather than event-style. AC3: add SPAN_ENCOUNTER_BEATS_SENT or document.
MISSING IN PYTHON — action-style field. AC3: add or document.

python_only_spans.rust_deviation: No production Rust emit site for component=combat found in mirror repo (only docstring examples). Python invented combat.* namespace for GM-panel per-tick visibility distinct from encounter.* domain events. ACCEPTED: Python-side convention, not drift.
Python-side convention; Rust has docstring example only. ACCEPTED.
Python-added validation-warning span; no Rust equivalent. ACCEPTED as Python-side enrichment.
